_comment: WC2026 Group B, MD3 (simultaneous). Standings before: Switzerland 4 pts (1-1 Qatar, 4-1 Bosnia), Canada 4 (1-1 Bosnia, 6-0 Qatar), Bosnia 1, Qatar 1. Top-of-group decider: a DRAW sends BOTH through (Bosnia/Qatar can reach only 4 and meet each other), so both are content with a point -> risk of a cagey, lower-scoring game; but both also want top spot for a better R32 draw. Near-even match, Canada a hair ahead (host, 6-0 form). LOW confidence. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Both on 4 points; winner takes the group, a draw qualifies both. Bosnia/Qatar can't catch them if they draw.

home.name: Switzerland
notes: Organized European side; beat Bosnia 4-1, drew Qatar. attack 1.12.
Content with a draw (qualifies) -> cagey, rho -0.08.

away.name: Canada
notes: Host nation, attacking form (6-0 Qatar, 1-1 Bosnia). attack 1.20, slight host edge.
Also happy with a draw -> low-block risk on both sides.

_postmatch.result: Switzerland 2-1 Canada. Both scored, 3 goals; Switzerland wins the group, Canada through as runner-up.
_postmatch.verdict: Flagged as a coin-flip with NO strong read (LOW). The slight 1X2 lean (Canada 38%) missed -- Switzerland (33.5%) won -- but that is exactly the kind of even game where the favourite tag is meaningless. The game-SHAPE read landed: I expected an open-ish game with BTTS live (53.9%) and it was 2-1, both scoring, 3 goals (Over 2.5). Both qualified regardless, as the model already had them at ~100% to advance.
_postmatch.lesson: On a true coin-flip, grade the process not the side: the open/BTTS read was right; the marginal 1X2 lean is noise. No calibration change.
